channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: IAF Seeks Air-Launched Loitering Decoy (ALLD-EW) for SEAD & DEAD Missions from Su-30MKI
item.description: India’s innovation push under iDEX has taken a major leap with ADITI 4.0, as the Indian Air Force (IAF) calls on the private sector to develop an advanced Air-Launched Loitering Decoy with Electronic Warfare (ALLD-EW) capability. Designed for deployment from the Su-30MKI, this system aims to transform how India conducts Suppression and Destruction of Enemy Air Defence (SEAD/DEAD) missions.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: CSIR-CSIO’s Breakthrough: India Moves Closer to Futuristic AR Combat Glasses with Holographic Waveguide Displays
item.description: India is on the verge of a significant leap in augmented reality (AR) technology as CSIR-CSIO, Chandigarh prepares to roll out indigenous holographic waveguide-based displays later this month. This development is being closely watched not just as a technological milestone, but as a potential game-changer for next-generation soldier systems and defence applications.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Army Seeks Combat-Ready Humanoid Robots to Reduce Soldier Risk
item.description: India’s push toward next-generation battlefield technologies has entered a bold new domain under iDEX ADITI 4.0, with the Indian Army calling for the development of a combat-capable humanoid autonomous system. The objective is clear: reduce human exposure to extreme risk by deploying intelligent machines capable of operating alongside—or even ahead of—soldiers in hostile environments.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Can Slash Su-57 Costs Through Domestic Production, Says IAF Veteran
item.description: India’s potential entry into the Su-57 fifth-generation fighter program could be significantly more cost-effective if the aircraft is manufactured domestically, according to Retired Group Captain Uttam Kumar Devnath. Drawing from India’s extensive legacy of producing Russian-origin fighters, Devnath argues that the economic and strategic case for local assembly and progressive indigenization is far stronger today than during earlier joint programs.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAF Launches program to Transform 100–120 kg Bombs into Long-Range Precision Weapons
item.description: India’s push to modernize its aerial strike capabilities continues to gain momentum as the Indian Air Force (IAF) introduces another key challenge under the ADITI initiative, focused on converting smaller 100–120 kg class bombs into long-range precision-guided weapons. This new problem statement highlights a strategic effort to enhance the effectiveness of existing munitions through advanced guidance and propulsion technologies.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: AMCA’s Invisible Edge: How Indigenous RCS Software Is Powering India’s Stealth Fighter Ambitions
item.description: India’s Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) program is not just about designing a fifth-generation fighter—it is about mastering the invisible domain of stealth. A critical enabler of this capability is indigenous Radar Cross Section (RCS) management and optimization software developed by Zeus Numerix Pvt Ltd for the Aeronautical Development Agency (ADA). This software is quietly playing a decisive role in shaping how stealth is designed, validated, and refined for India’s most ambitious fighter jet program.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pegasus V12 UAS Unveiled: India’s Jet-Powered Multi-Role Drone for Training and Combat Missions
item.description: Mysore-based Model Aerosports Private Limited (MASPL) has unveiled plans for the Pegasus V12 Unmanned Aerial System (UAS), a jet-powered platform designed to bridge the gap between training and operational combat roles. The system has been conceptualised in response to a recurring requirement from defence operators for a single adaptable platform capable of performing multiple mission profiles, eliminating the need for deploying separate drones for different tasks.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Proud moment: Modi on Kalpakkam fast breeder reactor attaining criticality
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Monday said India has taken a defining step in its civil nuclear journey as the indigenously built Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor at Kalpakkam attained criticality. In a post on X, Modi said this is a proud moment for the country. “Today, India takes a defining step in its civil nuclear journey, advancing the second stage of its nuclear programme,” he said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Army unveils technology roadmap for unmanned aerial systems, loitering munitions
item.description: The army on Monday unveiled a key “technology roadmap” outlining technical and operational requirements in the domain of unmanned aerial systems (UAS) and loitering munitions, a move that comes amid the changing nature of warfare as seen in various conflicts.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: No Talks With Iran On Hormuz Transit Levy, Reports Baseless: Govt
item.description: The government on Monday dismissed reports suggesting that India was in discussions with Iran over imposing a transit levy through the Strait of Hormuz, calling such claims baseless and without any foundation.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pakistan’s provinces locked in row over share in river waters after India cuts down supply
item.description: A major controversy is raging in Pakistan over the distribution of water among provinces, with the southern province of Sindh crying foul ahead of a crucial meeting of Pakistan’s water regulator for the allocation during the kharif season, local media reports said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Iran rejects latest ceasefire proposal as Trump deadline approaches
item.description: Iran on Monday rejected the latest ceasefire proposal and instead said it wants a permanent end to the war, as US President Donald Trump’s ultimatum loomed within hours.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indo-Japan science and technology cooperation one of strong pillars: MoS Jitendra Singh
item.description: Union Minister of State for Science and Technology, Jitendra Singh delivered a virtual address at the grand finale of the India-Japan Year of Science, Technology and Innovation Exchange, on Monday, held at the Embassy of India in Tokyo, highlighting four decades of robust bilateral cooperation.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: BCAS & RRU Pact: Aviation Security Equipment Testing
item.description: The Bureau of Civil Aviation Security and Rashtriya Raksha University have signed a pact to set up and operate an indigenous testing centre for performance evaluation and certification of full-body scanners and other security screening equipment used at airports, an official release said on Monday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Danish national detained for illegally entering restricted area along Indo-Pak border in Bikaner
item.description: A 40-year-old Danish national was detained near the India-Pakistan international border in Rajasthan’s Bikaner district for allegedly entering a restricted area without permission, police said on Sunday. The man, identified as Rene, has been sent to the Joint Interrogation Centre in Bikaner for detailed questioning, and his passport details were being verified, they said.
item.dc:creator: admin
